response:
Over the last 24 hours of Binance spot BTCUSDT (as of **2026-05-14T12:28:04Z**):

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Period open | 80,202.78 |
| Period close | 79,430.39 |
| Net change (USDT) | -772.39 |
| Net change (%) | -0.963 |
| Total 24h dollar volume | 1,290,454,362.90 |
| Mean hourly volume | 51,618,174.52 |
| Median hourly volume | 38,349,621.35 |
| Max hourly volume | 137,204,913.51 at 2026-05-13T15:00:00Z |
| Min hourly volume | 16,064,914.29 (latest hour) |
| Last 4h avg volume | 27,764,932.60 |
| Prior 4h avg volume | 45,552,882.27 |

**Price trend:** BTC drifted lower by roughly 0.96% (~$772) across the 24-hour window. The period low (~78,827.21) printed during the highest-volume hour.

**Volume trend:** Volume was front-loaded. The largest hourly flow (~137.2M USDT) hit on the dip to ~78,827.21. Since then, activity tapered sharply, with the most recent hour posting the thinnest liquidity of the entire window (~16.1M USDT).

**Volume expansion flag:** **No — volume did not expand into the latest move.** The last 4-hour average (~27.8M USDT/hr) is roughly 39% below the prior 4-hour average (~45.6M USDT/hr) and well under the 24-hour mean. The drift lower into the close is occurring on contracting, not expanding, flow.

A 24-hour price & volume chart was also rendered for reference.

developerTrace.query: For binance spot BTCUSDT, summarize the last 24 hours of BTC spot close_price and dollar_volume trend from Velo rows and flag whether volume expanded into the latest move.

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

rows = get_market_rows_1["rows"]
df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
df["time"] = pd.to_datetime(df["time"], unit="ms")
df = df.sort_values("time").reset_index(drop=True)

# Trend summary
price_change = df["close_price"].iloc[-1] - df["close_price"].iloc[0]
price_change_pct = price_change / df["close_price"].iloc[0] * 100
volume_total = df["dollar_volume"].sum()
volume_mean = df["dollar_volume"].mean()
volume_median = df["dollar_volume"].median()
volume_max = df["dollar_volume"].max()
volume_min = df["dollar_volume"].min()

# Volume into latest move: compare last 4 hours vs prior 4 hours vs overall mean
last4 = df["dollar_volume"].iloc[-4:].mean()
prior4 = df["dollar_volume"].iloc[-8:-4].mean()

# Identify the largest volume bar and its time
max_vol_idx = df["dollar_volume"].idxmax()
max_vol_time = df.loc[max_vol_idx, "time"]
max_vol_price = df.loc[max_vol_idx, "close_price"]

# Plot
fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax1.plot(df["time"], df["close_price"], color="#1f77b4", linewidth=2, label="Close Price")
ax1.set_ylabel("Close Price (USDT)", color="#1f77b4")
ax1.tick_params(axis="y", labelcolor="#1f77b4")
ax1.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x:,.0f}"))

ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax2.bar(df["time"], df["dollar_volume"], width=0.03, color="orange", alpha=0.4, label="Dollar Volume")
ax2.set_ylabel("Dollar Volume", color="orange")
ax2.tick_params(axis="y", labelcolor="orange")
ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x/1e6:.1f}M"))

# Highlight max volume bar
ax2.bar(max_vol_time, max_vol, width=0.03, color="red", alpha=0.7, label="Max Volume Hour")

plt.title("Binance Spot BTCUSDT — Last 24h Close Price & Dollar Volume")
fig.legend(loc="upper left", bbox_to_anchor=(0.1, 0.95))
plt.tight_layout()
save_figure(alt="Binance BTCUSDT 24h price and volume trend", title="BTCUSDT 24h Price & Volume")

# Result summary
result = {
    "price_start": float(df["close_price"].iloc[0]),
    "price_end": float(df["close_price"].iloc[-1]),
    "price_change_usdt": float(price_change),
    "price_change_pct": float(price_change_pct),
    "volume_total_24h": float(volume_total),
    "volume_mean": float(volume_mean),
    "volume_median": float(volume_median),
    "volume_max": float(volume_max),
    "volume_min": float(volume_min),
    "last_4h_avg_volume": float(last4),
    "prior_4h_avg_volume": float(prior4),
    "volume_expanded_latest": bool(last4 > prior4 and last4 > volume_mean),
    "max_volume_time": str(max_vol_time),
    "max_volume_price": float(max_vol_price),
    "max_volume": float(volume_max),
}
set_result(result)

postProcessingContract.operation: Compute 24h price/volume trend summary and flag volume expansion into latest move

                          ^^^^^^^
NameError: name 'max_vol' is not defined
. RETRY by calling code_interpreter again in your next step with corrected Python that fixes this exact error — do NOT call finalResponse yet, do NOT call any other tool, and do NOT silently drop the requested artifact. You have 4 more code_interpreter retry attempt(s) before the runtime force-stops the per-turn budget. Common Python fixes: import pandas/numpy/matplotlib at the top of the script; access prior data via its canonical _contextAlias (each alias is a Python global AND a key in the inputs dict — e.g. tool_method_1 and inputs["tool_method_1"] resolve to the same object), or via friendly local names if you declared them in args.inputs; use df.loc[]/df.iloc[] for indexed access; sort time series with df.sort_values(); call set_result({...}) at the end with a JSON-serializable dict so the agent can read the value back; call save_figure(alt=..., title=...) after building a matplotlib figure to emit an image artifact. NEVER import requests/urllib/httpx/yfinance/aiohttp — outbound network is blocked at the sandbox level and these libraries are not installed; all data must come from prior MCP tool calls.

args.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

rows = get_market_rows_1["rows"]
df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
df["time"] = pd.to_datetime(df["time"], unit="ms")
df = df.sort_values("time").reset_index(drop=True)

# Trend summary
price_change = df["close_price"].iloc[-1] - df["close_price"].iloc[0]
price_change_pct = price_change / df["close_price"].iloc[0] * 100
volume_total = df["dollar_volume"].sum()
volume_mean = df["dollar_volume"].mean()
volume_median = df["dollar_volume"].median()
volume_max = df["dollar_volume"].max()
volume_min = df["dollar_volume"].min()

# Volume into latest move: compare last 4 hours vs prior 4 hours vs overall mean
last4 = df["dollar_volume"].iloc[-4:].mean()
prior4 = df["dollar_volume"].iloc[-8:-4].mean()

# Identify the largest volume bar and its time
max_vol_idx = df["dollar_volume"].idxmax()
max_vol_time = df.loc[max_vol_idx, "time"]
max_vol_price = df.loc[max_vol_idx, "close_price"]
max_vol = volume_max  # FIX: define max_vol explicitly

# Plot
fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax1.plot(df["time"], df["close_price"], color="#1f77b4", linewidth=2, label="Close Price")
ax1.set_ylabel("Close Price (USDT)", color="#1f77b4")
ax1.tick_params(axis="y", labelcolor="#1f77b4")
ax1.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x:,.0f}"))

ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax2.bar(df["time"], df["dollar_volume"], width=0.03, color="orange", alpha=0.4, label="Dollar Volume")
ax2.set_ylabel("Dollar Volume", color="orange")
ax2.tick_params(axis="y", labelcolor="orange")
ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"${x/1e6:.1f}M"))

# Highlight max volume bar
ax2.bar(max_vol_time, max_vol, width=0.03, color="red", alpha=0.7, label="Max Volume Hour")

plt.title("Binance Spot BTCUSDT — Last 24h Close Price & Dollar Volume")
fig.legend(loc="upper left", bbox_to_anchor=(0.1, 0.95))
plt.tight_layout()
save_figure(alt="Binance BTCUSDT 24h price and volume trend", title="BTCUSDT 24h Price & Volume")

# Result summary
result = {
    "price_start": float(df["close_price"].iloc[0]),
    "price_end": float(df["close_price"].iloc[-1]),
    "price_change_usdt": float(price_change),
    "price_change_pct": float(price_change_pct),
    "volume_total_24h": float(volume_total),
    "volume_mean": float(volume_mean),
    "volume_median": float(volume_median),
    "volume_max": float(volume_max),
    "volume_min": float(volume_min),
    "last_4h_avg_volume": float(last4),
    "prior_4h_avg_volume": float(prior4),
    "volume_expanded_latest": bool(last4 > prior4 and last4 > volume_mean),
    "max_volume_time": str(max_vol_time),
    "max_volume_price": float(max_vol_price),
    "max_volume": float(volume_max),
}
set_result(result)

toolSchemas.description: Check Velo API connectivity and the velo-node client version. Use this first when validating the API key or diagnosing upstream availability.
List Velo futures products across supported exchanges. Supports filtering by exchange, product substring, coin/product text, and including delisted contracts.
List Velo spot products across supported exchanges. Useful for finding BTC, ETH, SOL, or exchange-specific spot pairs before requesting rows.
List Velo options products, currently centered on Deribit under the upstream API. Use before options IV/skew queries.
Return Velo's available futures, spot, and options data columns so the caller can request valid row metrics.

Fetch exact Velo market rows for futures, spot, or Deribit options. Use when the buyer asks for Velo rows, latest row values, OHLCV, close_price, dollar_volume, funding_rate, dollar_open_interest_close, buy/sell liquidation dollar volumes, options index_price, iv_1w, iv_1m, iv_3m, skew_1w, skew_1m, or dvol_close.

type.description: Velo row family: futures for perp metrics, spot for spot OHLCV, options for Deribit IV/skew/DVOL rows.

coins.description: Underlying coin filters such as BTC, ETH, or SOL; useful for options rows and cross-exchange futures rows.

columns.description: Exact Velo row columns to return, such as close_price, dollar_volume, funding_rate, dollar_open_interest_close, iv_1w, skew_1m, or dvol_close.

products.description: Exchange product symbols such as BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, SOLUSDT, or BTC-USDT-SWAP.

exchanges.description: Velo exchange names such as binance-futures, bybit, okex-swap, hyperliquid, binance, or deribit.

resolution.description: Velo row resolution such as 1m, 5m, 1h, 1d, or 1w.

lookbackHours.description: Relative lookback window when begin is omitted.

toolSchemas.description: Analyze Velo futures rows for funding, open-interest change, dollar volume, buy/sell liquidations, and liquidation imbalance. Use for futures leverage pressure across binance-futures, bybit, okex-swap, hyperliquid, BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, and SOLUSDT.

coins.description: Underlying coins to compare across futures venues, such as BTC, ETH, or SOL.

limit.description: Maximum futures rows to stream before summarizing.

products.description: Futures products such as BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, SOLUSDT, BTC-USDT-SWAP, or BTC-USD.

exchanges.description: Velo futures exchanges, for example binance-futures, bybit, okex-swap, or hyperliquid.

resolution.description: Velo row resolution such as 1h for hourly futures rows.

lookbackHours.description: Window for open-interest change, volume, and liquidation aggregation.

toolSchemas.description: Fetch Velo market-cap rows for selected coins. Use for current circ, circ_dollars, fdv, fdv_dollars, circulating supply, fully diluted valuation, or OI-to-market-cap leverage context.

coins.description: Coin symbols such as BTC, ETH, or SOL.

lookbackHours.description: Relative lookback window when begin is omitted.

toolSchemas.description: Fetch Velo futures term-structure rows for selected coins. Use for curve-shape questions about dte points, forward IV fields when returned, basis, carry, contango, backwardation, upward-sloping, or inverted term structure.

coins.description: Coin symbols such as BTC or ETH.

lookbackHours.description: Relative lookback window when begin is omitted.

toolSchemas.description: Fetch Velo level-2 order book depth snapshots by exchange/product or by coin. Use for order book depth, latest mid, executable liquidity, market-impact, slippage, and venue cost comparisons.

coin.description: Optional coin symbol for cross-exchange depth, such as BTC, ETH, or SOL.

limit.description: Maximum streamed depth rows to return.

product.description: Product symbol such as BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, SOLUSDT, BTC-USDT-SWAP, or BTC-USD.

exchange.description: Velo exchange name such as binance-futures, bybit, okex-swap, or hyperliquid.

resolution.description: Depth resolution in minutes, such as 1, 5, 10, 15, 30, or 60.

lookbackHours.description: Relative depth snapshot lookback when begin is omitted.

toolSchemas.description: Fetch recent Velo crypto news stories. Pair BTC-, ETH-, or SOL-tagged news with futures rows, market caps, depth, funding, liquidation, open-interest, or volatility analysis.

Build a compact Velo market memo for selected coins by combining futures structure, market caps, and recent news. Use for buyer-facing positioning or leverage summaries that need multiple Velo data families.
